title: 綜合黎睇 HKU vs ChinU

What I said in another post:



"Your statements are lacking of nutrients

In order to confidently claim University A is better than University B,

we shall be able to observe that: for the same subjects, the majority of A's admission scores are higher than those of B's.

This is what we can observe from comparing 3Big to non-3Big, so we can claim confidently that "3Big is better than non-3Big"

However according to the admission score list I've shown above, we can observe that: neither CUHK nor HKU has the majority of its subjects achieving higher admission scores than its rival.

Thus, we cannot conclude neither CUHK > HKU nor HKU > CUHK, only CUHK ~ HKU"



Since when did I say CUHK > HKU? on9 jai

Just tell me, when did I say CUHK > HKU?

What I said in another post:



"Your statements are lacking of nutrients

In order to confidently claim University A is better than University B,

we shall be able to observe that: for the same subjects, the majority of A's admission scores are higher than those of B's.

This is what we can observe from comparing 3Big to non-3Big, so we can claim confidently that "3Big is better than non-3Big"

However according to the admission score list I've shown above, we can observe that: neither CUHK nor HKU has the majority of its subjects achieving higher admission scores than its rival.

Thus, we cannot conclude neither CUHK > HKU nor HKU > CUHK, only CUHK ~ HKU"

What I said in another post:



"Your statements are lacking of nutrients

In order to confidently claim University A is better than University B,

we shall be able to observe that: for the same subjects, the majority of A's admission scores are higher than those of B's.

This is what we can observe from comparing 3Big to non-3Big, so we can claim confidently that "3Big is better than non-3Big"

However according to the admission score list I've shown above, we can observe that: neither CUHK nor HKU has the majority of its subjects achieving higher admission scores than its rival.

Thus, we cannot conclude neither CUHK > HKU nor HKU > CUHK, only CUHK ~ HKU"



Since when did I say CUHK > HKU? on9 jai

先不論你之前唔同post 踩hku抬高cu. 淨係cuhk～hku 已經要收收皮先

Sigh... Local students I tell you what,

For IB students, you are basically (90% of the time) guaranteed to get offers from both CUHK and HKU for the same subject (if both CUHK and HKU have that course)

e.g.

an IB student with CUHK Pharm offer must also have a HKU Pharm offer and vice versa.

an IB student with HKU BBA A&F offer must also have a CUHK PACC offer


So please tell me, if HKU is ABSOLUTELY 100% >>> CUHK, then why CUHK can have most of its subject being the score as HKU? For some subjects, CUHK has even a higher score

So what I am saying is: CUHK ~ HKU
because we cant see clearly that HKU > CUHK
